title: Matt Turner’s loan with the Revolution extended; New England has option to buy GK from Lyon
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Matt Turner's loan with New England Revolution from Lyon has been extended through December 31, 2026.
New England Revolution has an option to extend Turner's loan through 2027 and buy his contract outright.
Turner can be classified under Targeted Allocation Money (TAM), freeing cap space for potential new signings.

title: Trooper injured in wrong-way crash on Mass. Pike in Chicopee
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts State Trooper and K-9 injured in a wrong-way crash on Interstate 91 in Chicopee.
Driver Jose G Santiago Jr. charged with DUI, negligent operation, after a 0.19% breathalyzer result.
Trooper, K-9, and another driver expected to recover; incident prompts calls for legislative changes.

title: Here’s how hot and humid it could get this week, according to Boston meteorologists
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A Heat Advisory is in effect from 11 AM Tuesday to 8 PM Wednesday for southern New England.
Temperatures will reach the mid to upper 90s, feeling like 95-103°F with humidity.
Stronger risk of severe thunderstorms expected Tuesday in northern New England near the Canadian border.

title: Red Sox used a 9th round pick in MLB Draft on an intriguing power-hitting prospect
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Red Sox selected 18-year-old power-hitting outfielder Martin Shelar in the ninth round of the MLB Draft.
Shelar, from Georgia, hit .500 with 19 home runs last year and has a 115.5 mph exit velocity record.
There are concerns about Shelar's contact consistency, but his power has earned him comparisons to Jay Buhner.

title: A woman had to be rescued from a N.H. mountain. Then her boyfriend proposed.
author: John Waller
bullets: Alexis Hardy, injured while hiking Mount Kearsarge, was rescued by New Hampshire Fish and Game officers.
During the rescue operation, Tim Dupere proposed to Alexis Hardy, and she accepted.
Hardy was taken to Concord Hospital for treatment after being carried down the Rollins Trail.

title: Nurses are back at Brigham and Women’s Hospital, but the fight is not over
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Brigham and Women's Hospital's largest nursing strike in Massachusetts history ended after five days.
Despite ending the strike, nurses returned to work without a contract agreement after eight months of negotiations.
Mass General Brigham claims patient care continued smoothly despite the work stoppage and successful transition on Monday.

title: These are the beaches closed in Mass. as the latest heat wave hits
author: Peter Chianca and Heather Alterisio
bullets: Around 50 Massachusetts beaches closed due to high bacteria levels as heat wave approaches.
Beaches in Salisbury, Ipswich, and Plum Island are open after sewer main breaks resolved.
The state updates the beach water quality dashboard hourly between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. daily.

title: 17-year-old drowns in Fall River pond
author: John Waller
bullets: A 17-year-old boy drowned Sunday night in South Watuppa Pond in Fall River.
The teen was swimming to a floating dock when he struggled and did not return to shore.
State Police found the teen deceased after a search; foul play is not suspected.

title: Ranking Bruins’ top 10 prospects: Who stands out in Boston’s growing pipeline?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, Dean Letourneau, and Will Zellers lead Boston Bruins' prospect pool.
James Hagens is expected to play a larger role in the 2026-27 season.
Bruins' prospects like Nils Bartholdsson and Chris Pelosi show promise in collegiate and international leagues.

title: One of the few remaining privately owned Berkshire ‘cottages’ sold for $4 million
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Pine Needles 'cottage' in Lenox sold for $4 million, one of the last privately owned Berkshire estates.
Built in 1903, the 13,261-square-foot Colonial Revival sits on 80 acres, originally owned by the Blake family.
The property includes six bedrooms, eight bathrooms, a carriage house, and a tennis court, remaining a family residence.
